title: Challenges of the twenty-first century
description: Test your skills on the domestic and international challenges of the 21st century!

assessment_items.question_content:
> “On September the 11th, enemies of freedom committed an act of war against our country. Americans have known wars—but for the past 136 years, they have been wars on foreign soil, except for one Sunday in 1941. Americans have known the casualties of war—but not at the center of a great city on a peaceful morning. Americans have known surprise attacks—but never before on thousands of civilians. All of this was brought upon us in a single day—and night fell on a different world, a world where freedom itself is under attack. . . .”

> “We will direct every resource at our command—every means of diplomacy, every tool of intelligence, every instrument of law enforcement, every financial influence, and every necessary weapon of war—to the disruption and to the defeat of the global terror network.” 

> -Source: President George W. Bush, “Address to Congress after the Attacks on September 11,” 2001

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a calculated set of attacks on the World Trade Center and the Pentagon
an attack on a military base in Hawaii by Japanese fighter pilots
the detonation of a nuclear warhead off the coast of California
the interception of a telegram orchestrating an attack on the White House

Americans have known wars—but for the past 136 years, they have been wars on foreign soil, except for one Sunday in 1941. Americans have known the casualties of war—but not at the center of a great city on a peaceful morning. Americans have known surprise attacks—but never before on thousands of civilians. All of this was brought upon us in a single day—and night fell on a different world, a world where freedom itself is under attack. . . .”

> “We will direct every resource at our command—every means of diplomacy, every tool of intelligence, every instrument of law enforcement, every financial influence, and every necessary weapon of war—to the disruption and to the defeat of the global terror network.” 

> -Source: President George W. Bush, “Address to Congress after the Attacks on September 11,” 2001

**The events described in the excerpt contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the declaration of a War on Terror
the search for suspected terrorists in government
the buildup of nuclear and conventional weapons
the creation of the United Nations

assessment_items.question_content:
> “On September the 11th, enemies of freedom committed an act of war against our country. Americans have known wars—but for the past 136 years, they have been wars on foreign soil, except for one Sunday in 1941. Americans have known the casualties of war—but not at the center of a great city on a peaceful morning. Americans have known surprise attacks—but never before on thousands of civilians. All of this was brought upon us in a single day—and night fell on a different world, a world where freedom itself is under attack. . . .”

> “We will direct every resource at our command—every means of diplomacy, every tool of intelligence, every instrument of law enforcement, every financial influence, and every necessary weapon of war—to the disruption and to the defeat of the global terror network.” 

> -Source: President George W. Bush, “Address to Congress after the Attacks on September 11,” 2001

**The events discussed in the excerpt led to which of the following political changes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the United States launched military efforts in Afghanistan and Iraq
the government imposed a quota on immigration from the Middle East
the realignment of the two major political parties
the start of a series of proxy wars in South American and Asian countries

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0207a73e01c618b6d5b5c22e5823186b.png)
**Source: Michael Sauers**, [Flickr Creative Commons](https://www.flickr.com/photos/travelinlibrarian/73870599/)

**The image most directly illustrates a United States policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: improving security by increasing the government’s power of surveillance
interrogating all suspected terrorists at a military base in Guantanamo Bay
investigating people who had ties to the Communist Party or the Soviet Union
instituting search requirements in airports to prevent another attack

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0207a73e01c618b6d5b5c22e5823186b.png)
**Source: Michael Sauers**, [Flickr Creative Commons](https://www.flickr.com/photos/travelinlibrarian/73870599/)

**The policy suggested in the image was most directly a response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a set of terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center and Pentagon
a law that increased the government’s power of surveillance
a declaration of war on Afghanistan and Iraq due to terrorist groups
a rise in undocumented immigration from Latin American countries

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0207a73e01c618b6d5b5c22e5823186b.png)
**Source: Michael Sauers**, [Flickr Creative Commons](https://www.flickr.com/photos/travelinlibrarian/73870599/)

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the passage of a law that increased surveillance powers for law enforcement and intelligence agencies
the commitment of military forces to Iraq and Afghanistan to search for members of terrorist groups
the reduction in funding for local and state libraries in an effort to balance the budget
the discovery of plans by domestic terrorist cells to bomb several public libraries

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Seven years ago, we made the single biggest investment in clean energy in our history. Here are the results. In fields from Iowa to Texas, wind power is now cheaper than dirtier, conventional power. On rooftops from Arizona to New York, solar is saving Americans tens of millions of dollars a year on their energy bills, and employs more Americans than coal — in jobs that pay better than average. We’re taking steps to give homeowners the freedom to generate and store their own energy. . . . Meanwhile, we’ve cut our imports of foreign oil by nearly sixty percent, and cut carbon pollution more than any other country on Earth.”

> -Source: President Barack Obama, State of the Union address, 2016

**The excerpt most directly reflects the United States’s efforts to do which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: reduce American dependence on fossil fuels
punish oil-producing countries in the Middle East
recover after a major economic recession
create new jobs to combat unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Seven years ago, we made the single biggest investment in clean energy in our history. Here are the results. In fields from Iowa to Texas, wind power is now cheaper than dirtier, conventional power. On rooftops from Arizona to New York, solar is saving Americans tens of millions of dollars a year on their energy bills, and employs more Americans than coal — in jobs that pay better than average. We’re taking steps to give homeowners the freedom to generate and store their own energy. . . . Meanwhile, we’ve cut our imports of foreign oil by nearly sixty percent, and cut carbon pollution more than any other country on Earth.”

> -Source: President Barack Obama, State of the Union address, 2016

**The ideas in the excerpt about energy have the most in common with ideas associated with which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the conservation movement in the 1900s
the Populist Movement in the 1890s
the abolition movement in the 1850s
the utopian movement in the 1830s

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Seven years ago, we made the single biggest investment in clean energy in our history. Here are the results. In fields from Iowa to Texas, wind power is now cheaper than dirtier, conventional power. On rooftops from Arizona to New York, solar is saving Americans tens of millions of dollars a year on their energy bills, and employs more Americans than coal — in jobs that pay better than average. We’re taking steps to give homeowners the freedom to generate and store their own energy. . . . Meanwhile, we’ve cut our imports of foreign oil by nearly sixty percent, and cut carbon pollution more than any other country on Earth.”

> -Source: President Barack Obama, State of the Union address, 2016

**Which of the following groups would have been most likely to support Obama’s views as expressed in this excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: members of the Green Party
miners in the coal industry
leaders of oil companies
members of the Republican Party

assessment_items.question_content:
“American decline is real, though the apocalyptic version of it reflects the familiar ruling-class perception that anything short of total control amounts to total disaster. Despite the [[☃ definition 1]] [[☃ definition 2]], the United States remains the world’s dominant power by a large margin, with no competitor in sight, and not only in the military dimension, in which, of course, the United States reigns supreme.” 

> -Source: Noam Chomsky, historian, “Who Rules the World?” 2016

**Which of the following most likely contributed to the rise in belief of the “American decline” referred to in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Great Recession of 2008, a period of global financial crisis that affected the American economy
the search for and subsequent death of Osama Bin Laden, who led Al-Qaeda during September 11
the September 11 attacks, which included orchestrated attacks on the World Trade Center and the Pentagon
the election of Barack Obama, the first black man to serve as President of the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
“American decline is real, though the apocalyptic version of it reflects the familiar ruling-class perception that anything short of total control amounts to total disaster. Despite the [[☃ definition 1]] [[☃ definition 2]], the United States remains the world’s dominant power by a large margin, with no competitor in sight, and not only in the military dimension, in which, of course, the United States reigns supreme.” 

> -Source: Noam Chomsky, historian, “Who Rules the World?” 2016

**According to the passage, which of the following is one important continuity in the status of the United States from 1990 to 2019?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States remained the world’s leading superpower.
The United States competed for superpower status with the Soviet Union.
The United States was replaced as the dominant superpower by China.
The United States lost superpower status following the Cold War.

assessment_items.question_content:
“American decline is real, though the apocalyptic version of it reflects the familiar ruling-class perception that anything short of total control amounts to total disaster. Despite the [[☃ definition 1]] [[☃ definition 2]], the United States remains the world’s dominant power by a large margin, with no competitor in sight, and not only in the military dimension, in which, of course, the United States reigns supreme.” 

> -Source: Noam Chomsky, historian, “Who Rules the World?” 2016

**Which of the following statements best describes the author’s argument in the passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States continued to dominate as a global superpower despite economic and foreign policy challenges.
The United States lost its status as a dominant superpower after the Great Recession of 2008.
The United States became vulnerable to other emerging superpowers, like China and Russia.
The United States returned to isolationism after the Cold War, relinquishing its status as a global superpower.
